sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Clicking on the yellow container at (31, 11) did NOT change it. The yellow container still has 23 pixels and the same boundary. But clicking on the light green container at (10, 29) DID change it.
What's the difference? Let me think...
- The light green container is 3 pixels wide (cols 28-32) and 3 pixels tall (rows 9-11)
- The yellow container is 3 pixels wide (cols 9-11) and 8 pixels tall (rows 28-35)
I clicked at (31, 11) on the yellow container. Col 11 is the rightmost column of the yellow container. Maybe the click needs to be on a specific part of the container?
Wait, let me reconsider. When I clicked at (10, 29) on the light green container:
- The light green container was at cols 28-32 (5 wide)
- I clicked at col 29, which is the 2nd column from the left
- The result was that the container shrank to cols 28-29 (2 wide)
So it seems like clicking cuts the container at the clicked column, keeping the left portion.
For the yellow container, I clicked at (31, 11):
- The yellow container is at cols 9-11 (3 wide)
- Col 11 is the rightmost column
- Maybe clicking on the rightmost column doesn't cut anything because there's nothing to the right?
Let me try clicking at a different position on the yellow container, like (31, 10) which is the middle column.
Actually wait - let me reconsider the light green container click. The light green container had boundary [[9, 28], [9, 32], [11, 32], [11, 28]]. After clicking at (10, 29), it became [[9, 28], [9, 29], [11, 29], [11, 28], [10, 29]]. 
Hmm, the boundary format is a bit confusing. Let me look at the ASCII:
Before:
```
row 9: BBGNNNNNBB  (cols 27=G, 28-32=NNNNN)
row 10: BBGNNNrNBB (cols 27=G, 28-29=NN, 30=r, 31=N... wait)
```
Actually, let me re-read the ASCII more carefully. The initial state showed:
```
row 9: BBGNNNNNBB
row 10: BBGNNNrNBB
row 11: BBGNNNNNBB
```
This is from cols 25-34. So:
- col 25-26: BB
- col 27: G
- col 28-32: NNNNN (5 wide)
- col 33-34: BB
Wait, that's only 10 characters for cols 25-34, which is 10 columns. Let me count:
BBGNNNNNBB = 10 chars
So cols 25-34: B, B, G, N, N, N, N, N, B, B
That means:
- col 25: B
- col 26: B
- col 27: G
- col 28: N
- col 29: N
- col 30: N
- col 31: N
- col 32: N
- col 33: B
- col 34: B
Wait, but the red dot was at (10, 31). Looking at row 10:
BBGNNNrNBB = 10 chars
- col 25: B
- col 26: B
- col 27: G
- col 28: N
- col 29: N
- col 30: N
- col 31: r
- col 32: N
- col 33: B
- col 34: B
So the red dot is at col 31, and the light green rectangle spans cols 28-32 (with the red dot replacing one N at col 31).
After clicking at (10, 29):
```
row 9: BBGNNBBBBB
row 10: BBGrNBBBBB
row 11: BBGNNBBBBB
```
- col 25: B
- col 26: B
- col 27: G
- col 28: N
- col 29: N (row 9), r (row 10), N (row 11)
- col 30-34: B
So the light green rectangle now spans cols 28-29 (2 wide), and the red dot moved to (10, 28).
Wait, but (10, 29) was a light green pixel. After clicking, it became:
- (10, 28): r (red dot)
- (10, 29): N (light green)
So the red dot moved from (10, 31) to (10, 28), and the light green rectangle shrank from cols 28-32 to cols 28-29.
The click was at (10, 29), which was a light green pixel. The result was that everything to the right of the click was removed, and the red dot moved to the leftmost position.
Now for the yellow container, I clicked at (31, 11), which is the rightmost column. Since there's nothing to the right, maybe nothing was cut. Let me try clicking at a more central position.
Actually, I realize I should look at the yellow container more carefully. It's 3 columns wide (cols 9-11) and 8 rows tall (rows 28-35). The red dot is at (34, 10).
Let me try clicking at (31, 10) which is the middle column.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The yellow container didn't change when I clicked at col 11 (rightmost).
# Let me try clicking at col 10 (middle column) of the yellow container

result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 10}])
print(f"Result: board_changed={result.get('board_changed')}")

# Check the yellow container after click
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Yellow container after click ===")
for r in range(26, 37):
    print(f"  row {r}: {ascii_lines[r][7:14]}")

# Check node 16
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] == 16:
        print(f"\nNode 16: px={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['id'] == 17:
        print(f"Node 17 (red dot): boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Result: board_changed=True

=== Yellow container after click ===
  row 26: BBBBBBB
  row 27: BBGGGBB
  row 28: BBYYYBB
  row 29: BBYYYBB
  row 30: BBYYYBB
  row 31: BBYYYBB
  row 32: BBYYYBB
  row 33: BBYYYBB
  row 34: BBYrYBB
  row 35: BBYYYBB
  row 36: BBBBBBB

Node 16: px=23, boundary=[[28, 9], [28, 11], [35, 11], [35, 9]]
Node 17 (red dot): boundary=[[34, 10]]
